text: If \( P(n) \): \( 7^n - 3^n \) is divisible by 4, then at \( n=2 \): \( 7^2 - 3^2 = \)
explanation: \( 49 - 9 = 40 = 4 \times 10 \). Divisible by 4. ✓
name: Divisibility Verification
subject: maths

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: Area enclosed between curves \( y=f(x) \) and \( y=g(x) \) where \(f(x)\geq g(x)\) is:
explanation: Area between two curves: \( \int_a^b [f(x)-g(x)]\,dx \) when \(f \geq g\) on \([a,b]\).
name: Area Between Curves
subject: maths

text: The temperature coefficient of resistance \(\alpha\) is defined by:
explanation: \( R_T = R_0(1+\alpha\Delta T) \). For metals: \(\alpha > 0\) (resistance increases with T). For semiconductors: \(\alpha < 0\).
name: Temperature Coefficient of Resistance
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( 2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0 \), then \( \alpha \cdot \beta = \)
explanation: Product of roots \( = \frac{c}{a} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Product of Roots
subject: maths

text: Faraday's first law of electromagnetic induction states:
options: Whenever magnetic flux through a circuit changes, an EMF is induced
Magnetic field is proportional to current
explanation: Faraday's 1st law: changing magnetic flux induces EMF. 2nd law: induced EMF \( = -\frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} \) (Faraday-Neumann law).
name: Faraday's First Law
subject: phy

text: The Arrhenius equation relating rate constant to temperature is:
explanation: Arrhenius equation: \( k = Ae^{-E_a/RT} \), where \(A\) = frequency factor, \(E_a\) = activation energy.
name: Arrhenius Equation
subject: che

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: n-type semiconductors are formed by doping with:
explanation: n-type: doped with Group 15 element (5 valence electrons) → extra electron → electron-rich (negative carriers).
name: n-type Semiconductor
subject: che

text: Dehydration of alcohols to alkenes requires:
options: Conc. H₂SO₄ at ~170°C (excess acid, high temperature)
explanation: Dehydration (elimination): alcohol + conc. H₂SO₄ at 170°C → alkene (excess H₂SO₄). At 140°C with 2 mol alcohol → ether (Williamson synthesis).
name: Dehydration of Alcohols
subject: che

text: The intercept form of a plane is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1 \), where \(a,b,c\) are x,y,z intercepts.
name: Plane Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The conditional probability \( P(A|B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A|B) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)} \), provided \( P(B) \neq 0 \).
name: Conditional Probability
subject: maths

text: According to Aufbau principle, the order of orbital filling is:
explanation: Aufbau (n+l rule): 4s (n+l=4) fills before 3d (n+l=5). Order: 1s, 2s, 2p, 3s, 3p, 4s, 3d, 4p...
name: Aufbau Principle
subject: che

text: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^n x\,dx = \int_0^{\pi/2}\cos^n x\,dx \). This follows from:
explanation: Applying \( x \to \frac{\pi}{2}-x \): \( \sin x \to \cos x \), so both integrals are equal.
name: sin-cos Symmetry
subject: maths

text: The relation \( r = 4R\sin\frac{A}{2}\sin\frac{B}{2}\sin\frac{C}{2} \) connects:
explanation: This is the standard formula relating inradius \(r\) and circumradius \(R\) with half-angles.
name: r and R Relation
subject: maths

text: The cofactor \( C_{11} \) of matrix \( \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 4 & 5 \\ 1 & 0 & 6 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
name: Cofactor Calculation
subject: maths

text: The principal value range of \( \cos^{-1}x \) is:
explanation: \( \cos^{-1}x \) has principal value range \( [0, \pi] \).
name: Range of cos⁻¹
subject: maths

text: If \( P(A)=\frac{1}{2}, P(B)=\frac{1}{3} \) and \(A, B\) are independent, then \( P(A \cap B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{6} \)
name: Independent Events Intersection
subject: maths

text: The condition for constructive interference is path difference:
explanation: Constructive interference: \( \Delta = n\lambda \) (n = 0,±1,±2,...). Destructive: \( \Delta = (2n+1)\lambda/2 \).
name: Constructive Interference YDSE
subject: phy

text: If two roots of \( x^3 - px^2 + qx - r = 0 \) are equal, say \( \alpha, \alpha, \beta \), then which condition must hold?
explanation: From Vieta's: \( 2\alpha+\beta=p \), \( \alpha^2+2\alpha\beta=q \), \( \alpha^2\beta=r \). When all three equal: \( p^3=27r \). All conditions can hold simultaneously.
name: Equal Roots in Cubic
subject: maths

text: Power is defined as:
options: Work done per unit time: \( P = W/t = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \)
explanation: Power \( P = \frac{dW}{dt} = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \). SI unit: Watt (W) = J/s.
name: Definition of Power
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = \)
explanation: Pythagorean identity: \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1 \) for all \(\theta\).
name: Pythagorean Identity
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

text: Which process is represented by a vertical line on a P-V diagram?
explanation: Isochoric (constant volume): vertical line on P-V diagram. Isobaric (constant pressure): horizontal line. Isothermal: hyperbola (PV=const).
name: P-V Diagram Processes
subject: phy

text: The radius of the nth Bohr orbit of hydrogen is:
explanation: Bohr radius: \( r_n = n^2 a_0 \), where \( a_0 = 0.529\) Å. Ground state (n=1): 0.529 Å. n=2: 2.116 Å.
name: Bohr Orbit Radius
subject: phy

text: The lens formula is:
explanation: Thin lens formula: \( \frac{1}{v} - \frac{1}{u} = \frac{1}{f} \). New Cartesian sign convention. Positive \(f\) = converging; negative \(f\) = diverging.
name: Thin Lens Formula
subject: phy

text: The most stable oxidation state of iron (Fe) is:
explanation: Fe shows +2 (Fe²⁺, ferrous) and +3 (Fe³⁺, ferric). Fe³⁺ ([Ar]3d⁵) is particularly stable (half-filled d). Both are common and stable.
name: Stable Oxidation States of Fe
subject: che

text: The potential due to a dipole at a point on its equatorial line (at distance \(r\)) is:
explanation: On equatorial line: potential \( V = 0 \). (Equal and opposite contributions from +q and -q). Field is non-zero but potential is zero.
name: Dipole Potential on Equatorial Line
subject: phy

text: Beats are produced when two sound waves of frequencies \(f_1\) and \(f_2\) are superposed. Beat frequency equals:
explanation: Beat frequency \( f_{beat} = |f_1 - f_2| \). Perceptible as beats when difference is small (< ~7 Hz for human ear).
name: Beat Frequency
subject: phy

text: The projection of \( \vec{a} \) on \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Projection of \(\vec{a}\) on \(\vec{b}\): \( \frac{\vec{a}\cdot\vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|} = \vec{a}\cdot\hat{b} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Projection of Vector
subject: maths

text: The number of 4th roots of unity is:
explanation: The equation \( z^4 = 1 \) has exactly 4 roots: \( 1, -1, i, -i \)
name: Roots of Unity Count
subject: maths

text: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Using \( I = \int_0^{\pi/2}\frac{\sqrt{\cos x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}}dx \) by King's property, \(2I = \frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi}{4}\).
name: Classic Definite Integral
subject: maths

explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{a^2+x^2}dx = \frac{1}{a}\tan^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Standard Form Integral
subject: maths

text: The equation of a line with slope \(m\) and y-intercept \(c\) is:
explanation: Slope-intercept form: \( y = mx + c \)
name: Slope-Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The second law of thermodynamics implies:
options: Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot body (Clausius statement)
explanation: 2nd Law (Clausius): heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. (Kelvin-Planck): impossible to convert all heat to work.
name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: The iodoform test (CHI₃, yellow precipitate) is positive for:
explanation: Positive iodoform test: CH₃COR (methyl ketones), CH₃CH₂OH (ethanol), CH₃CHOH–R (secondary alcohols with CH₃ adjacent). Gives CHI₃ (yellow).
name: Iodoform Test
subject: che

text: The DE representing all parabolas with axis parallel to y-axis is of order:
explanation: General parabola \( y=ax^2+bx+c \) has 3 arbitrary constants. Differentiating 3 times gives order 3.
name: Order from Parabola Family
subject: maths

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
explanation: \( f(x) = \sqrt{2}\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{4}) \). Maximum \( = \sqrt{2} \) when \( x = \frac{\pi}{4} \).
name: Maximum of sin+cos
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Hyperbola (transverse axis along x): \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \). Eccentricity \(e > 1\).
name: Hyperbola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{2x+3}{(x^2+1)(x-2)} \) contains:
explanation: For an irreducible quadratic factor \(x^2+1\), the numerator in the partial fraction is linear: \(Ax+B\).
name: Irreducible Quadratic Factor
subject: maths

text: The value of \( A \) in \( \frac{1}{(x-1)(x+3)} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+3} \) is:
explanation: At \(x=1\): \( 1 = A(4) \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{4} \)
name: Coefficient by Cover-up
subject: maths

text: The Chain Rule: if \( y = f(g(x)) \), then \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Chain Rule: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = f'(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \)
name: Chain Rule
subject: maths

text: Wolff-Kishner reduction converts ketone to alkane using:
explanation: Wolff-Kishner: R₂C=O + NH₂NH₂ → R₂C=NNH₂ (hydrazone), then KOH/Δ → R₂CH₂ + N₂. Base conditions (complementary to Clemmensen).
name: Wolff-Kishner Reduction
subject: che

text: The shielding effect is maximum for which electrons?
explanation: s-electrons have maximum penetration to nucleus and thus maximum shielding effect. Shielding: s > p > d > f.
name: Shielding Effect
subject: che

text: Sandmeyer reaction converts diazonium salt to aryl halide using:
explanation: Sandmeyer: ArN₂⁺ + CuCl → ArCl + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuBr → ArBr + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuCN → ArCN + N₂. Replaces N₂ with X or CN.
name: Sandmeyer Reaction
subject: che

text: The coordination number of Co in [Co(en)₂Cl₂]⁺ (en = ethylenediamine) is:
explanation: en is a bidentate ligand (occupies 2 positions). 2 en × 2 = 4 positions + 2 Cl = 6. Coordination number = 6.
name: Coordination Number with Bidentate
subject: che

text: The electric field due to an infinite line charge with linear charge density \(\lambda\) at distance \(r\) is:
explanation: Using Gauss's law for cylindrical symmetry: \( E = \frac{\lambda}{2\pi\epsilon_0 r} \).
name: Electric Field - Line Charge
subject: phy

text: The function \( f(x) = |x| \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = |-x| = |x| = f(x) \). So \(f\) is an even function.
name: Even or Odd Function
subject: maths

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆
subject: che

text: The anomalous expansion of water means:
options: Water contracts from 0°C to 4°C (maximum density at 4°C)
explanation: Anomalous expansion: water contracts from 0°C to 4°C (density increases to max at 4°C), then expands normally above 4°C. Important for aquatic life in winter.
name: Anomalous Expansion of Water
subject: phy

text: The angular magnification of an astronomical telescope is:
explanation: Astronomical telescope: \( M = f_o/f_e \). Large \(f_o\) and small \(f_e\) → high magnification. Length \( = f_o + f_e \) (image at infinity).
name: Telescope Magnification
subject: phy

text: The parallel axis theorem states:
explanation: Parallel axis theorem: \( I = I_{cm} + Md^2 \), where \(d\) = distance from CM axis to new parallel axis.
name: Parallel Axis Theorem
subject: phy

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths

text: The modulus of rigidity (shear modulus) \(G\) is defined as:
explanation: Modulus of rigidity: \( G = \frac{\text{shear stress}}{\text{shear strain}} \). Liquids have G = 0 (cannot sustain shear).
name: Modulus of Rigidity
subject: phy

text: Which of the following is a factor of \( x^3 - 7x + 6 \)?
explanation: Test \(x=2\): \( 8 - 14 + 6 = 0 \). So \( (x-2) \) is a factor by Factor Theorem.
name: Factor Theorem Application
subject: maths

text: The average kinetic energy of a gas molecule at temperature \(T\) is:
explanation: Average translational KE per molecule = \( \frac{3}{2}kT \), where \( k = 1.38\times10^{-23}\,\text{J/K} \) (Boltzmann constant).
name: Average KE of Gas Molecule
subject: phy

text: The general solution of \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \Rightarrow \cos 2\theta = \cos 2\alpha \Rightarrow 2\theta = 2n\pi \pm 2\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \pm \alpha \)
name: sin²θ = sin²α
subject: maths

text: If the side of a square increases at 2 cm/s, the rate of increase of area when side = 5 cm is:
explanation: \( A=x^2 \Rightarrow \frac{dA}{dt} = 2x\frac{dx}{dt} = 2(5)(2) = 20 \text{ cm}^2/\text{s} \)
name: Related Rates
subject: maths

text: Metallic character down a group:
explanation: Metallic character increases down a group (easier to lose electrons due to larger size, lower IE). Cs is most metallic alkali metal.
name: Metallic Character Trend
subject: che

text: The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality states: \( (a_1^2+a_2^2)(b_1^2+b_2^2) \geq \)
name: Cauchy-Schwarz
subject: maths

text: The horizontal component of velocity of a projectile:
explanation: In projectile motion (no air resistance): horizontal velocity \(v_x = u\cos\theta\) = constant. Only vertical component changes due to gravity.
name: Horizontal Velocity in Projectile
subject: phy

text: If \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B \) is called:
explanation: By definition, if \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B = A^{-1} \), the inverse of \(A\).
name: Matrix Inverse Definition
subject: maths

text: The reaction of alkanes with Cl₂ in UV light is:
explanation: Halogenation of alkanes: free radical substitution. Initiation: Cl₂ → 2Cl• (UV). Propagation: Cl• + CH₄ → CH₃• + HCl, then CH₃• + Cl₂ → CH₃Cl + Cl•.
name: Halogenation of Alkanes
subject: che

text: Resonance in a forced oscillation occurs when:
explanation: Resonance: when driving frequency \( \omega_d = \omega_0 \) (natural frequency). Amplitude becomes maximum. Can cause structural failure.
name: Resonance Condition
subject: phy

text: The formula to calculate molar mass from freezing point depression is:
explanation: \( M_2 = \frac{K_f \times w_2 \times 1000}{\Delta T_f \times w_1} \), where \(w_1\) = mass of solvent (g), \(w_2\) = mass of solute (g).
name: Molar Mass from FP Depression
subject: che

text: The troposphere extends from Earth's surface to approximately:
explanation: Troposphere: 0–12 km (approx). Contains 75% of atmosphere's mass. Weather occurs here. Temperature decreases with altitude.
name: Troposphere Height
subject: che

explanation: Set \( x=1 \): \( \frac{1+1}{1 \cdot 0+...} \to 2 = C \cdot \frac{1}{1} \Rightarrow C = 2 \)
name: Repeated Factor Constant
subject: maths

text: Plaster of Paris is:
explanation: Plaster of Paris: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \) (hemi-hydrate). Sets by absorbing water: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \to \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \).
name: Plaster of Paris
subject: che

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \frac{1}{x} \) for \( x > 0 \).
name: Derivative of ln x
subject: maths

text: The general term (\( T_{r+1} \)) in the expansion of \( (x+y)^n \) is:
explanation: By Binomial Theorem: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{n}{r} x^{n-r} y^r \)
name: General Term Formula
subject: maths

text: K₂Cr₂O₇ in acidic medium: the half reaction at cathode involves Cr going from:
explanation: \( \text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-} + 14\text{H}^+ + 6e^- \to 2\text{Cr}^{3+} + 7\text{H}_2\text{O} \). Cr: +6 → +3 (reduction).
name: K₂Cr₂O₇ Reduction
subject: che

text: According to MO theory, which molecule is paramagnetic?
explanation: \(\text{O}_2\) MO configuration has 2 unpaired electrons in \(\pi^*\) orbitals → paramagnetic. This was a triumph of MO theory.
name: Paramagnetic Molecule
subject: che

text: A skew-symmetric matrix satisfies:
explanation: A matrix is skew-symmetric if \( A^T = -A \), i.e., \( A + A^T = 0 \).
name: Skew-Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: Carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) is given by:
options: Primary amines (1°) to give foul-smelling isocyanide
explanation: Carbylamine test: 1° amine + CHCl₃ + KOH → isocyanide (R–NC), very unpleasant smell. Positive only for primary amines. Test for 1° amines.
name: Carbylamine Test
subject: che

text: The radius of circular motion of a charged particle in a magnetic field is:
explanation: Circular motion in \(B\): centripetal force = magnetic force: \( \frac{mv^2}{r} = qvB \Rightarrow r = \frac{mv}{qB} \). Used in mass spectrometer.
name: Radius of Circular Motion in B
subject: phy

text: Molarity is defined as:
options: Moles of solute per kg of solvent
Moles of solute per litre of solution
Mass of solute per litre of solution
explanation: Molarity \( M = \frac{\text{moles of solute}}{\text{volume of solution in litres}} \)
name: Molarity Definition
subject: che

text: PET (polyethylene terephthalate) is a:
options: Polyester formed from ethylene glycol + terephthalic acid
explanation: PET: condensation polymerisation of ethylene glycol (HOCH₂CH₂OH) + terephthalic acid (1,4-benzenedicarboxylic acid). Used in bottles, fibres (Dacron, Terylene).
name: PET Formation
subject: che

text: Interhalogen compounds like ClF₃ are formed because:
options: Larger halogens can expand their octet using d orbitals
explanation: Interhalogens: formed between two different halogens. Larger halogens (Cl, Br, I) use d orbitals to accommodate more F atoms. E.g., IF₇.
name: Interhalogen Compounds
subject: che

text: The Nernst equation for electrode potential is:
explanation: Nernst equation: \( E = E^\circ - \frac{RT}{nF}\ln Q = E^\circ - \frac{0.0592}{n}\log Q \) at 25°C.
name: Nernst Equation
subject: che

text: The sphere with center \((a,b,c)\) and radius \(r\) has equation:
explanation: Sphere equation: \( (x-a)^2+(y-b)^2+(z-c)^2=r^2 \)
name: Equation of Sphere
subject: maths

text: The quartile deviation is defined as:
explanation: Quartile Deviation (semi-interquartile range) \( = \frac{Q_3 - Q_1}{2} \)
name: Quartile Deviation
subject: maths

text: \( \int \sin x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \sin x\,dx = -\cos x+C \)
name: Integral of sin
subject: maths

text: The order of stability of carbocations is:
explanation: Carbocation stability: 3° > 2° > 1° > methyl. More alkyl groups → more +I effect → better stabilisation of positive charge.
name: Carbocation Stability
subject: che

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: The Hall effect is used to determine:
options: Sign of charge carriers and carrier density: \( R_H = 1/(ne) \)
explanation: Hall effect: when current-carrying conductor is in \(\vec{B}\), transverse voltage (Hall voltage) develops. Determines sign of carriers and \( n = 1/(eR_H) \).
name: Hall Effect
subject: phy

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum
subject: maths

text: The critical angle \(\theta_c\) for TIR is:
explanation: Critical angle: \( \sin\theta_c = n_2/n_1 \) (\(n_1 > n_2\)). For glass-air: \( \theta_c \approx 42° \). At \(\theta_i = \theta_c\), refracted ray is at 90°.
name: Critical Angle
subject: phy

text: In a right triangle with right angle at \(C\), then \( c^2 = \)
explanation: When \( C = 90°, \cos C = 0 \): \( c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab(0) = a^2 + b^2 \). Pythagorean theorem.
name: Pythagoras from Cosine Rule
subject: maths

text: In the AC generator, the EMF produced is:
explanation: AC generator: \( \varepsilon = NBA\omega\sin(\omega t) = \varepsilon_0\sin(\omega t) \), where \(\varepsilon_0 = NBA\omega\) is peak EMF.
name: AC Generator EMF
subject: phy

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: The intercept form of a straight line is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{b} = 1 \), where \(a\) = x-intercept, \(b\) = y-intercept.
name: Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: For \( f(x) = x\sin\frac{1}{x} \), \( \lim_{x\to 0}f(x) = \)
explanation: \( |x\sin\frac{1}{x}| \leq |x| \to 0 \). By squeeze theorem, \( \lim_{x\to 0}x\sin\frac{1}{x} = 0 \).
name: Oscillating Limit
subject: maths

text: The real part of \( \frac{1}{1-\cos\theta + i\sin\theta} \) is:
explanation: After rationalizing and simplifying using half-angle identities, the real part equals \( \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Real Part Extraction
subject: maths

text: The relation between E and B amplitudes in an EM wave is:
explanation: \( E_0/B_0 = c \) (speed of light). The electric field amplitude is much larger than magnetic amplitude numerically.
name: E and B Amplitudes in EM Wave
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \cos^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) = \)
name: cos⁻¹(-1/2)
subject: maths

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: The Sandwich theorem (Squeeze theorem) states: if \( g(x)\leq f(x)\leq h(x) \) and \( \lim g = \lim h = L \), then:
explanation: Squeeze Theorem: if \(f\) is squeezed between \(g\) and \(h\), and both converge to \(L\), then \(\lim f = L\).
name: Squeeze Theorem
subject: maths

text: Brewster's angle (polarising angle) \(\theta_B\) is related to refractive index by:
explanation: Brewster's law: \( \tan\theta_B = n \) (\(n\) = refractive index of second medium). At this angle, reflected light is completely plane-polarised.
name: Brewster's Law
subject: phy

explanation: \( ^nC_r = ^nC_s \Rightarrow r+s=n \) (when \(r \neq s\)). So \( 12+8=n=20 \)
name: Equal Combinations Condition
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
explanation: Optical rotation depends on the nature of solute (chiral molecules), not on the number of particles.
name: Non-Colligative Property
subject: che

text: The work-energy theorem states:
options: Net work done on a body = change in kinetic energy
explanation: Work-Energy Theorem: \( W_{net} = \Delta KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 - \frac{1}{2}mu^2 \).
name: Work-Energy Theorem
subject: phy

text: The variation of \(g\) with depth \(d\) inside Earth is:
explanation: Inside Earth: \( g' = g\left(1 - \frac{d}{R}\right) \). At centre (d=R): g'=0. Decreases linearly going inside.
name: g Inside Earth
subject: phy

text: The C–X bond energy order (bond strength) among halogens is:
explanation: C–F bond is strongest (short, due to small F). Bond energy: C–F(485) > C–Cl(327) > C–Br(285) > C–I(213) kJ/mol.
name: C–X Bond Energy Order
subject: che

text: The speed of a transverse wave on a string with tension \(T\) and linear mass density \(\mu\) is:
explanation: Wave speed on string: \( v = \sqrt{T/\mu} \). Higher tension or lower mass density → faster wave.
name: Wave Speed on String
subject: phy

text: The angle \(\theta\) between lines with direction cosines \( (l_1,m_1,n_1) \) and \( (l_2,m_2,n_2) \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = l_1l_2+m_1m_2+n_1n_2 \). Lines are perpendicular when this equals 0.
name: Angle Between Lines in 3D
subject: maths

text: Lindlar's catalyst reduces alkynes to:
explanation: Lindlar's catalyst (Pd/CaCO₃ + quinoline): partial hydrogenation of alkynes gives cis-alkenes (syn addition of H₂). Na/liquid NH₃ gives trans-alkenes.
name: Lindlar's Catalyst
subject: che

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
subject: maths

text: Projection formula: \( a = \)
explanation: Projection formula: \( a = b\cos C + c\cos B \)
name: Projection Formula
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution tanθ=tanα
subject: maths

text: The accuracy of a Vernier calliper with least count 0.1 mm is:
explanation: Least count of Vernier calliper = 1 MSD − 1 VSD. Typical value: 0.1 mm = 0.01 cm. This is the smallest measurement it can make.
name: Vernier Calliper Least Count
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of a circle with center \((h,k)\) and radius \(r\) is:
explanation: Standard form: \( (x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2 \). When center is origin: \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \).
name: Standard Circle Equation
subject: maths

text: The reaction of HCHO (formaldehyde) with excess NaOH is:
explanation: HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro: 2HCHO + NaOH → CH₃OH (methanol) + HCOONa (sodium formate). Cross-Cannizzaro also possible.
name: HCHO Cannizzaro
subject: che

text: For hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), the asymptotes are:
explanation: Asymptotes of hyperbola: \( y = \pm\frac{b}{a}x \) (pass through center).
name: Asymptotes of Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: Van de Graaff generator is based on:
options: Electrostatic induction and the fact that charge distributes on outer surface of conductor
explanation: Van de Graaff generator: charge sprayed on belt, carried to hollow sphere where it distributes on outer surface. Can build up very high voltages (MV range).
name: Van de Graaff Generator Principle
subject: phy

text: DDT (dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) is:
explanation: DDT: chlorinated insecticide, non-biodegradable (persists in environment, accumulates in food chain). Banned in most countries now.
name: DDT
subject: che

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference
subject: maths

text: For \( K_{sp}(\text{Ag}_2\text{CrO}_4) \) and molar solubility \(s\):
name: Ksp of Ag₂CrO₄
subject: che

text: In an octahedral complex, the d orbital splitting gives:
options: All 5 d orbitals at same energy
explanation: Octahedral: d orbitals split into t₂g (dxy, dyz, dxz — lower, 3 orbitals) and eg (dx²-y², dz² — higher, 2 orbitals). Δo = energy gap.
name: Octahedral d Splitting
subject: che

text: The radius of gyration \(k\) is defined by:
explanation: Radius of gyration: \( k = \sqrt{I/M} \), so \( I = Mk^2 \). It represents the effective distance of the mass from the axis.
name: Radius of Gyration
subject: phy

text: The apparent weight of a person in a lift accelerating upward is:
explanation: Apparent weight: \( W' = m(g+a) \) when accelerating up (heavier feeling). \( W' = m(g-a) \) when accelerating down.
name: Apparent Weight in Lift
subject: phy

text: Newton's law of universal gravitation states: \( F = \)
explanation: Newton's gravitation: \( F = \frac{Gm_1m_2}{r^2} \), where \( G = 6.67\times10^{-11}\,\text{N m}^2/\text{kg}^2 \).
name: Newton's Law of Gravitation
subject: phy

text: The general solution of \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{x}{y} \) is:
explanation: Separate: \( y\,dy = x\,dx \Rightarrow \frac{y^2}{2}=\frac{x^2}{2}+C_1 \Rightarrow y^2=x^2+C \)
name: Separable Variables
subject: maths

text: The sum \( 1^3 + 2^3 + \cdots + n^3 = \)
explanation: \( \sum_{k=1}^n k^3 = \left[\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right]^2 \). The square of the sum of first \(n\) naturals.
name: Sum of Cubes
subject: maths

text: The angle between \( \vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j} \) and \( \vec{b}=\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) is:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = 0+1+0=1 \). \( |\vec{a}|=\sqrt{2}, |\vec{b}|=\sqrt{2} \). \( \cos\theta = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=60° \)
name: Angle Between Vectors
subject: maths

text: The radical axis of \( S_1=0 \) and \( S_2=0 \) is:
explanation: Radical axis: \( S_1 - S_2 = 0 \), which eliminates \(x^2\) and \(y^2\) terms.
name: Radical Axis Equation
subject: maths

text: Gauss's law for magnetism states:
explanation: Gauss's law for magnetism: \( \oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{A} = 0 \). Magnetic field lines form closed loops (no magnetic monopoles exist).
name: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
subject: phy

text: The pair of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: The lines are perpendicular when \( a + b = 0 \) (sum of coefficients of \(x^2\) and \(y^2\) is zero).
name: Perpendicular Pair
subject: maths

text: The electric field on the axial line of a dipole at large distance \(r\) is:
explanation: Axial field: \( E_{axial} = \frac{2p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \) (along dipole axis). Equatorial: \( E_{eq} = \frac{p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \). Ratio = 2:1.
name: Dipole Axial Field
subject: phy

text: The number of terms in the expansion of \( (a+b)^n \) is:
explanation: The expansion of \( (a+b)^n \) has \( n+1 \) terms, from \( T_1 \) to \( T_{n+1} \).
name: Number of Terms
subject: maths

text: The slope of the \(x\)-axis is:
explanation: The \(x\)-axis is horizontal: slope \( = 0 \). The \(y\)-axis is vertical: slope undefined (\(\infty\)).
name: Slope of Axes
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin 75° = \)
explanation: \( \sin 75° = \sin(45°+30°) = \frac{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{2}}{4} = \frac{\sqrt{3}+1}{2\sqrt{2}} \). Both are equivalent.
name: sin 75°
subject: maths

text: The equation \( \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \). Here \( a=4, b=3 \), so \( c = \)
explanation: \( c^2 = a^2-b^2 = 16-9 = 7 \Rightarrow c = \sqrt{7} \)
name: Foci of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: \( \cos 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \cos 3\theta = 4\cos^3\theta - 3\cos\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \), then \( \frac{1}{\alpha} + \frac{1}{\beta} = \)
explanation: \( \frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta} = \frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha\beta} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Sum of Reciprocals
subject: maths

text: At the mean (equilibrium) position in SHM:
options: Velocity is zero, acceleration is maximum
Velocity is maximum, acceleration is zero
explanation: At mean position: \( x=0 \Rightarrow a = -\omega^2 x = 0 \), and \( v = A\omega \) (maximum). At extreme position: \( v=0 \), \( a=A\omega^2 \) (max).
name: SHM at Mean Position
subject: phy

text: Choke coil (used to control AC without power loss) has:
options: Low resistance, high inductance (high reactance, low power factor)
explanation: Choke coil: high \(L\), low \(R\). High \(X_L = \omega L\) limits AC current with minimal heat loss. Power factor ≈ 0, very efficient AC current controller.
name: Choke Coil
subject: phy

text: The truth table of an OR gate: output is 1 when:
explanation: OR gate: output = 1 if any input = 1. Output = 0 only when ALL inputs = 0. Boolean: \( Y = A+B \) (logical sum).
name: OR Gate Truth Table
subject: phy

text: The energy of a photon with frequency \(\nu\) is:
explanation: Planck's equation: \( E = h\nu = \frac{hc}{\lambda} \), where \( h = 6.626 \times 10^{-34} \) J·s.
name: Planck's Equation
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{3x^2+2x}{5x^2-1} = \)
explanation: Divide numerator and denominator by \(x^2\): \( \frac{3+2/x}{5-1/x^2} \to \frac{3}{5} \)
name: Rational Limit at Infinity
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: \( \sinh x = \)
explanation: Definition: \( \sinh x = \frac{e^x - e^{-x}}{2} \)
name: Definition of sinh
subject: maths

text: The enthalpy of formation of water (\(\Delta H^\circ_f\)) at 25°C is approximately:
explanation: \( \text{H}_2(g) + \frac{1}{2}\text{O}_2(g) \to \text{H}_2\text{O}(l) \), \(\Delta H^\circ_f = -286\) kJ/mol (exothermic).
name: Enthalpy of Formation of Water
subject: che

text: The Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of a ketone with a peracid gives:
options: Ester (inserts oxygen into C–C bond next to carbonyl)
explanation: Baeyer-Villiger: R–CO–R' + peracid (mCPBA) → R–COO–R' (ester). An oxygen atom is inserted between carbonyl C and one alkyl group.
name: Baeyer-Villiger Oxidation
subject: che

text: The number of moles in 44 g of \( \text{CO}_2 \) (molar mass = 44 g/mol) is:
explanation: \( n = \frac{\text{mass}}{\text{molar mass}} = \frac{44}{44} = 1 \text{ mol} \)
name: Moles Calculation
subject: che

text: The displacement in the \(n\)th second of uniformly accelerated motion is:
explanation: \( s_n = u + \frac{a}{2}(2n-1) \). This is displacement in the nth second specifically.
name: Displacement in nth Second
subject: phy

text: Charles' Law states that at constant pressure:
explanation: Charles' Law: \( V \propto T \) at constant \(P\), i.e., \( \frac{V_1}{T_1} = \frac{V_2}{T_2} \). T must be in Kelvin.
name: Charles' Law
subject: che

text: The potentiometer is used to measure EMF because it draws:
explanation: Potentiometer: null method — at balance, no current flows through galvanometer. Gives accurate EMF without drawing current from source.
name: Potentiometer Principle
subject: phy

text: Chemisorption compared to physisorption has:
options: Lower activation energy and lower \(\Delta H\)
Higher activation energy and higher \(\Delta H\)
explanation: Chemisorption: chemical bond formation, high \(E_a\), high \(\Delta H_{ads}\) (40-400 kJ/mol), monolayer, irreversible.
name: Chemisorption vs Physisorption
subject: che

text: The number of ways to arrange the letters of MISSISSIPPI is:
explanation: MISSISSIPPI has M:1, I:4, S:4, P:2. Arrangements \( = \frac{11!}{4!4!2!} \)
name: Repeated Letters Arrangement
subject: maths

text: Area of circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) using integration is:
explanation: Area \( = 4\int_0^r \sqrt{r^2-x^2}\,dx = \pi r^2 \). Verified by standard integration.
name: Area of Circle
subject: maths

text: Two lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: Lines are perpendicular when \( m_1 \cdot m_2 = -1 \).
name: Perpendicular Lines
subject: maths

text: If \( \omega \) is a cube root of unity, then \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = \)
explanation: The sum of all cube roots of unity is always 0: \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0 \)
name: Cube Roots of Unity
subject: maths

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1) \) to line \( ax+by+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to line: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+c|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2}} \)
name: Point-Line Distance
subject: maths

text: An LED (Light Emitting Diode) emits light due to:
options: Recombination of electrons and holes releasing photons (electroluminescence)
explanation: LED: electron-hole recombination in forward-biased p-n junction releases energy as photon. Color depends on bandgap energy. More efficient than incandescent bulbs.
name: LED Working Principle
subject: phy

text: If \( f(x) = x^3 \), then \( f \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = (-x)^3 = -x^3 = -f(x) \). So \(f\) is an odd function.
name: Odd Function Check
subject: maths

text: The solubility product \(K_{sp}\) of \( \text{AgCl} \) is related to molar solubility \(s\) by:
explanation: \(\text{AgCl} \rightleftharpoons \text{Ag}^+ + \text{Cl}^-\): \([\text{Ag}^+]=[\text{Cl}^-]=s\). \(K_{sp} = s \cdot s = s^2\).
name: Ksp and Solubility
subject: che

text: For the data \( \{1, 1, 1, 1, 1\} \), the standard deviation is:
explanation: All values are equal to 1, so every deviation from mean is 0. Variance = 0, SD = 0.
name: SD of Constant Data
subject: maths

text: The molecularity of a reaction is:
options: Number of reactant molecules in elementary step
explanation: Molecularity = number of molecules colliding in a single elementary step. It is always a whole number (1, 2, or 3).
name: Molecularity
subject: che

text: Degree of dissociation \(\alpha\) of a weak electrolyte by conductivity is:
explanation: \( \alpha = \frac{\Lambda_m}{\Lambda^\infty_m} \). As dilution increases, \(\alpha \to 1\) (complete dissociation at infinite dilution).
name: Degree of Dissociation from Conductivity
subject: che

text: If \( [\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}] = 0 \), the vectors are:
explanation: Zero scalar triple product means the three vectors are coplanar (lie in the same plane).
name: Coplanar Vectors
subject: maths
